evidence.claim: Used Haystack, PEFT, Pinecone, Time Series, Machine Learning, PyTorch, Flask, Qdrant, Kubeflow, LangChain, Deep Learning, NLP, GANs, BM25, OpenSearch, Elasticsearch, and REST APIs
evidence.source_text: Skills: Haystack (advanced, 25mo, 54 endorsements); PEFT (expert, 84mo, 15 endorsements); Pinecone (expert, 65mo, 46 endorsements); Time Series (advanced, 33mo, 35 endorsements); Machine Learning (expert, 78mo, 41 endorsements); PyTorch (advanced, 28mo, 49 endorsements); Flask (intermediate, 30mo, 7 endorsements); Qdrant (advanced, 46mo, 14 endorsements); Kubeflow (intermediate, 17mo, 14 endorsements); LangChain (advanced, 40mo, 50 endorsements); Deep Learning (advanced, 34mo, 40 endorsements); NLP (expert, 58mo, 34 endorsements); GANs (intermediate, 33mo, 0 endorsements); BM25 (advanced, 47mo, 0 endorsements); OpenSearch (expert, 36mo, 34 endorsements); Elasticsearch (expert, 42mo, 52 endorsements); REST APIs (intermediate, 15mo, 5 endorsements)

evidence.claim: Built content recommendation system serving 10M+ users at Google
evidence.source_text: Built a content recommendation system serving 10M+ users

evidence.claim: Improved 7-day retention by 6% and time spent per session by 14% at Google
evidence.source_text: The launch improved 7-day retention by 6% and time spent per session by 14%.

evidence.claim: Evolved ranking layer from hand-tuned scoring to learning-to-rank model
evidence.source_text: Owned the ranking layer for an e-commerce search product, evolving it from a hand-tuned scoring function to a learning-to-rank model over 9 months.

evidence.claim: Prioritizes evaluation rigor and offline benchmarks
evidence.source_text: I care a lot about evaluation rigor — too many teams ship models without offline benchmarks they trust.

evidence.claim: Values practical experience over academic theory
evidence.source_text: My academic background is in CS/ML but my main learning has come from shipping real systems and seeing what holds up under production load.
